reasoning: Strong opportunity for 2-16x speedup across all queries. P1 (zero-copy mmap) eliminates critical I/O bottleneck (500-1000ms per query). P2 (zone maps) enables 80-90% block skipping. Both are foundational optimizations with clear payoff. With 2 iterations remaining, risk tolerance is high.
focus_areas: Zero-copy mmap access (eliminate vector copy anti-pattern)
Zone maps with block-level pruning on l_shipdate and o_orderdate
Perfect hash aggregation for Q1 (direct array indexing)
Top-K min-heap for Q3 (replace full sort)
strategy_notes: Prioritize P1 and P2 as they are synergistic and foundational. P1 (zero-copy) must be completed first as it changes storage API - all queries depend on it. P2 (zone maps) requires reingest, so implement it now while we have iteration budget. P3 and P4 are low-risk algorithmic improvements that complement the I/O optimizations. Expected outcome: Q1→850ms (2.2x), Q3→2200ms (2.2x), Q6→75ms (16.9x). This sets up iteration 2 for SIMD vectorization and parallel execution.
notes: IMPORTANT: Implement optimizations in this order: (1) P1 first - modify storage.h/cpp to use raw pointers instead of vectors, update all three queries to use pointer-based access. (2) P2 second - implement zone map generation during ingestion, add block-skipping logic to queries. (3) P3 and P4 are independent and can be done in parallel. P2 requires reingest=true, so ensure ingest.cpp changes are complete before running evaluation. Test incrementally - validate P1 before moving to P2.
